revision.comment: 新しいページ：小盛山古墳（岡山県岡山市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 小盛山古墳 |画像 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]平山 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 42|緯度秒 = 2.5 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m |築造年代 = [[4世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |史跡指定 = なし |特記事項 = 円墳としては全国第2位の規模 |地図=Japan Okayama |ラベル = 小盛山古墳 |アイコン=円墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''小盛山古墳'''（こもりやまこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]平山にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。史跡指定はされていない。
円墳としては全国で第2位の規模の古墳である。別名を「造山古墳（つくりやまこふん）」とも（岡山市北区新庄下の[[造山古墳 (岡山市)|造山古墳]]とは異なる）{{Sfn|小盛山古墳（平凡社）|1988年}}。
== 概要 == 岡山市西方の山裾の丘陵端に築造された大型円墳である{{Sfn|小盛山古墳（平凡社）|1988年}}。墳形は円形で、[[造出]]を付す<ref name="岡山市"/>。墳丘は3段築成で、造出部分は2段築成<ref name="岡山市"/>。直径は95メートル、造出まで含めた墳丘長は108メートルを測るが、これは円墳としては全国第2位の規模になる<ref name="岡山市"/>。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]が検出されている<ref name="岡山市"/>。墳丘周囲に残る4つの溜池は周濠の名残と伝えるが、詳細は明らかでない<ref name="岡山市"/>。主体部の埋葬施設は不明{{Sfn|小盛山古墳（平凡社）|1988年}}。
この小盛山古墳は、[[古墳時代]]前期後半の[[4世紀]]後半の築造と推定される<ref name="岡山市">[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html 小盛山古墳]（岡山市「岡山シティミュージアム」）。</ref>。前期の円墳としては全国で最大規模になる<ref name="岡山市"/>。吉備地方の前期古墳としては、小盛山古墳以前の築造になる[[尾上車山古墳]]・[[中山茶臼山古墳]]が知られるが、両古墳が吉備の中山の山上に位置して海を臨むのとは性格を異にする点が注目される<ref name="岡山市"/>。なお本古墳に後続する首長墓としては、[[佐古田堂山古墳]]・[[造山古墳 (岡山市)|造山古墳]]と続く系譜が推測される<ref>[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html 佐古田堂山古墳]（岡山市「岡山シティミュージアム」）。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=小盛山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|小盛山古墳（平凡社）|1988年}}}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html 小盛山古墳] - 岡山市「岡山シティミュージアム」
{{DEFAULTSORT:こもりやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]]

{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}
[[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}} {{Infobox Ancient Japanese Burial Mound |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoue Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Round tumuli]] [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

{{Infobox Ancient Japanese Burial Mound |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Round tumuli]] [[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Round tumuli]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Round tumuli]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Round tumuli]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Round tumuli]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Komoriyama Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Komoriyama Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Komoriyama Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Komoriyama Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Komoriyama Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Komoriyama Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama {{Infobox ancient site |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama {{Infobox ancient site |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama {{Infobox ancient site |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama {{Infobox ancient site |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama {{Infobox ancient site |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama {{Infobox ancient site |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References ==
