{{Infobox ancient site |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]

revision.comment: moving the kofun template to the bottom of each page
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |construction_period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its shape is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama
