revision.comment: trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation" [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designated as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 42|latitude_seconds = 2.5 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 50|longitude_seconds = 7.46 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |label = Komoriyama Kofun |icon = Round tumulus |label_position = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not designation1 as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting platform). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinatesonds = 2.5 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 7.46 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_map_dot_label = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not other_designation1 as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Plattype-style projection}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_other_designation"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_other_designation"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinatesonds = 2.5 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 7.46 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not other_designation1 as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Plattype-style projection}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinatesonds = 2.5 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 7.46 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not other_designation1 as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Plattype-style projection}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Plattype-style projection}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinatesonds = 2.5 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 7.46 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not other_designation1 as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Plattype-style projection}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinatesonds = 2.5 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 7.46 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not other_designation1 as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Plattype-style projection}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinatesonds = 2.5 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 7.46 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not other_designation1 as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Plattype-style projection}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation" [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinatesonds = 2.5 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 7.46 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not other_designation1 as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinatesonds = 2.5 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 7.46 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Komoriyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]] [[sn:omoriyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Komoriyama Kofun}}

In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|12=draft}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|12=draft}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Komoriyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]] [[sn:omoriyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Komoriyama Kofun}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|12=draft}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|12=draft}} Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinatesonds = 2.5 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 7.46 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}} |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳|12=draft}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}. It is a round tumulus and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市|12=draft}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|12=draft}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=draft}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=draft}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|12=draft}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|12=draft}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Komoriyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]] [[sn:omoriyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Komoriyama Kofun}}
