Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|12=draft}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|12=draft}} Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinatesonds = 2.5 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 7.46 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}} |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳|12=draft}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}. It is a round tumulus and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市|12=draft}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|12=draft}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=draft}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=draft}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|12=draft}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|12=draft}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Komoriyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]] [[sn:omoriyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Komoriyama Kofun}}

This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市|12=draft}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|12=draft}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=draft}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=draft}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|12=draft}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|12=draft}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Komoriyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]] [[sn:omoriyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Komoriyama Kofun}}

It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|12=draft}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Komoriyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]] [[sn:omoriyama Kofun]] {{citation needed|Komoriyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|12=draft}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|12=draft}} Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinatesonds = 2.5 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 7.46 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}} |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳|12=draft}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}. It is a round tumulus and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市|12=draft}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|12=draft}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=draft}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=draft}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|12=draft}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|12=draft}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]]{{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Komoriyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]] [[sn:omoriyama Kofun]] {{citation needed|Komoriyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|12=draft}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|12=draft}} Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinates = 2.5 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 7.46 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}} |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳|12=draft}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}. It is a round tumulus and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. This tumulus ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市|12=draft}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|12=draft}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=draft}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=draft}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Among early period round tumuli, it ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|12=draft}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|12=draft}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Komoriyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Komoriyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]] [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[sn:omoriyama Kofun]]

Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|12=draft}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|12=draft}} Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinates = 2.5 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 7.46 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}} |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳|12=draft}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}. It is a round tumulus and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. This tumulus Ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}} {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市|12=draft}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|12=draft}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=draft}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=draft}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Among early period round tumuli, it Ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|12=draft}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|12=draft}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Komoriyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Komoriyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]] [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[sn:omoriyama Kofun]]

revision.comment: 9 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:小盛山古墳 up to rev 101786624
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 小盛山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
墳丘全景 |所属 = |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]平山 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 42|緯度秒 = 2.5 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 7.46 |ISO = JP-33 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |史跡 = なし |特記事項 = 円墳としては全国第3位の規模 |地図=Japan Okayama |ラベル = 小盛山古墳 |アイコン=円墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''小盛山古墳'''（こもりやまこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]平山にある[[古墳]]。形状は円墳。史跡指定はされていない。 円墳としては全国で第3位の規模の古墳である全国の主な円墳は次の通り。 # [[富雄丸山古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]） - 直径109メートル。 # [[丸墓山古墳]]（[[埼玉県]][[行田市]]） - 直径105メートル。 # '''小盛山古墳'''（岡山県岡山市） - 直径95メートル。。別名を「造山古墳（つくりやまこふん）」とも（岡山市北区新庄下の[[造山古墳]]とは異なる）{{Sfn|小盛山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 == 概要 == 岡山市西方の山裾の丘陵端に築造された大型円墳である{{Sfn|小盛山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。墳形は円形で、[[造出]]を付す。墳丘は3段築成で、造出部分は2段築成。直径は95メートル、造出まで含めた墳丘長は108メートルを測り、円墳としては全国第3位の規模になる。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]が検出されている。墳丘周囲に残る4つの溜池は周濠の名残と伝えるが、詳細は明らかでない。埋葬施設は不明{{Sfn|小盛山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 この小盛山古墳は、[[古墳時代]]前期後半の[[4世紀]]後半頃の築造と推定される[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html 小盛山古墳]（岡山市シティミュージアム「[https://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/bunkazai_top/index.html 岡山市の文化財探訪]」）。。前期の円墳としては全国で最大規模になる。吉備地方の前期古墳としては、小盛山古墳以前の築造になる[[尾上車山古墳]]・[[中山茶臼山古墳]]が知られるが、両古墳が吉備の中山の山上に位置して海を臨むのとは性格を異にする点が注目される。なお本古墳に後続する首長墓としては、[[佐古田堂山古墳]]・[[造山古墳]]と続く系譜が推測される[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html 佐古田堂山古墳]（岡山市シティミュージアム「[https://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/bunkazai_top/index.html 岡山市の文化財探訪]」）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''注釈''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=小盛山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|小盛山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html 小盛山古墳] - 岡山市シティミュージアム「[https://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/bunkazai_top/index.html 岡山市の文化財探訪]」 {{DEFAULTSORT:こもりやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]] [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]] Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinates = 2.5 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 7.46 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komoriyama Kofun|qq=}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. This tumulus Ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]) - Diameter: 109 meters. # [[:en:Maruhakayama Kofun|Maruhakayama Kofun]] ([[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] [[:en:Gyōda|Gyōda]]) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the [[:en:4th century|4th century]], during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun period|qq=}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Among early period round tumuli, it Ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as [[:en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]] and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakotadōyama Kofun|qq=}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小盛山古墳|version=101786624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]] [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[sn:omoriyama Kofun]]

Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]] [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]] Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinates = 2.5 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 7.46 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = top }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komoriyama Kofun|qq=}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. This tumulus Ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]) - Diameter: 109 meters. # [[:en:Maruhakayama Kofun|Maruhakayama Kofun]] ([[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] [[:en:Gyōda|Gyōda]]) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the [[:en:4th century|4th century]], during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun period|qq=}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Among early period round tumuli, it Ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as [[:en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]] and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakotadōyama Kofun|qq=}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小盛山古墳|version=101786624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]] [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[sn:omoriyama Kofun]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]]

It is a round tumulus and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. This tumulus Ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]) - Diameter: 109 meters. # [[:en:Maruhakayama Kofun|Maruhakayama Kofun]] ([[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] [[:en:Gyōda|Gyōda]]) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the [[:en:4th century|4th century]], during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Among early period round tumuli, it Ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as [[:en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]] and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakotadōyama Kofun|qq=}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小盛山古墳|version=101786624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]] [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[sn:omoriyama Kofun]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]]

In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as [[:en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]] and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakotadōyama Kofun|qq=}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小盛山古墳|version=101786624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Komoriyama Kofun]] [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[sn:omoriyama Kofun]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]]

[[ja:小盛山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28685137]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]] [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]] Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinates = {{Coord|34.700694|N|133.835406|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |coordinatesrees =133|omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747}} }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komoriyama Kofun|qq=}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. This tumulus Ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|qq=|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]) - Diameter: 109 meters. # [[:en:Maruhakayama Kofun|Maruhakayama Kofun]] ([[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] [[:en:Gyōda|Gyōda]]) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the [[:en:4th century|4th century]], during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Among early period round tumuli, it Ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as [[:en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]] and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakotadōyama Kofun|qq=}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小盛山古墳|version=101786624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q28685137}}

It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakotadōyama Kofun|qq=}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小盛山古墳|version=101786624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q28685137}}

[[ja:小盛山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28685137]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]] [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]] Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinates = {{Coord|34.700694|N|133.835406|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |coordinatesrees =133|omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komoriyama Kofun|qq=}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. This tumulus Ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]) - Diameter: 109 meters. # [[:en:Maruhakayama Kofun|Maruhakayama Kofun]] ([[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] [[:en:Gyōda|Gyōda]]) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the [[:en:4th century|4th century]], during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Among early period round tumuli, it Ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as [[:en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]] and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakotadōyama Kofun|qq=}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小盛山古墳|version=101786624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q28685137}}

In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as [[:en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]] and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakotadōyama Kofun|qq=}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小盛山古墳|version=101786624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q28685137}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:小盛山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28685137]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]] [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]] Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinates = {{Coord|34.700694|N|133.835406|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |coordinatesrees =133|omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komoriyama Kofun|qq=}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. This tumulus Ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]) - Diameter: 109 meters. # [[:en:Maruhakayama Kofun|Maruhakayama Kofun]] ([[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] [[:en:Gyōda|Gyōda]]) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the [[:en:4th century|4th century]], during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Among early period round tumuli, it Ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as [[:en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]] and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakotadōyama Kofun|qq=}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小盛山古墳|version=101786624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q28685137}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]
