It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakotadōyama Kofun|qq=}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小盛山古墳|version=101786624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q28685137}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:小盛山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28685137]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]] [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]] Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinates = {{Coord|34.700694|N|133.835406|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |coordinatesrees =133|omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komoriyama Kofun|qq=}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. This tumulus Ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]) - Diameter: 109 meters. # [[:en:Maruhakayama Kofun|Maruhakayama Kofun]] ([[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] [[:en:Gyōda|Gyōda]]) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the [[:en:4th century|4th century]], during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Among early period round tumuli, it Ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as [[:en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]] and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakotadōyama Kofun|qq=}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小盛山古墳|version=101786624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q28685137}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as [[:en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]] and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakotadōyama Kofun|qq=}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小盛山古墳|version=101786624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q28685137}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:小盛山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28685137]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]] [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]] Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinates = {{Coord|34.700694|N|133.835406|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |coordinatesrees =133|omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komoriyama Kofun|qq=}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. This tumulus Ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]) - Diameter: 109 meters. # [[:en:Maruhakayama Kofun|Maruhakayama Kofun]] ([[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] [[:en:Gyōda|Gyōda]]) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the [[:en:4th century|4th century]], during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Among early period round tumuli, it Ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as [[:en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]] and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakotadōyama Kofun|qq=}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小盛山古墳|version=101786624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q28685137}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as [[:en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]] and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakotadōyama Kofun|qq=}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小盛山古墳|version=101786624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{wikidata link|Q28685137}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]] [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]] Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinates = {{Coord|34.700694|N|133.835406|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |coordinatesrees =133|omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komoriyama Kofun|qq=}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. This tumulus Ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]) - Diameter: 109 meters. # [[:en:Maruhakayama Kofun|Maruhakayama Kofun]] ([[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] [[:en:Gyōda|Gyōda]]) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the [[:en:4th century|4th century]], during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Among early period round tumuli, it Ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as [[:en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]] and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakotadōyama Kofun|qq=}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小盛山古墳|version=101786624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{wikidata link|Q28685137}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakotadōyama Kofun|qq=}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小盛山古墳|version=101786624|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{wikidata link|Q28685137}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:小盛山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28685137]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}} {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama}} {{ill|北區|en|北區|WD=Q1180096|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)}} Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinates = {{Coord|34.700694|N|133.835406|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |coordinatesrees =133|omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century}} |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komoriyama Kofun|qq=}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|北區|en|北區|WD=Q1180096|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)}}, {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a round tumulus and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. This tumulus Ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|en|Maruhakayama Kofun|WD=Q11368078|lt=Maruhakayama Kofun}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}} {{ill|Gyōda|en|Gyōda|WD=Q860659|lt=Gyōda}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|en|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|WD=Q11639745|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Among early period round tumuli, it Ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|en|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|WD=Q28685132|lt=Onoe Kurumayama Kofun}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakotadōyama Kofun|qq=}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小盛山古墳|version=101786624|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{wikidata link|Q28685137}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|en|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|WD=Q28685132|lt=Onoe Kurumayama Kofun}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakotadōyama Kofun|qq=}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小盛山古墳|version=101786624|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{wikidata link|Q28685137}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:小盛山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Komoriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Komoriyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}} {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama}} {{ill|北區|en|北區|WD=Q1180096|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)}} Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinates = {{Coord|34.700694|N|133.835406|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |coordinatesrees =133|omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century}} |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komoriyama Kofun|qq=}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|北區|en|北區|WD=Q1180096|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)}}, {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a round tumulus and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. This tumulus Ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|en|Maruhakayama Kofun|WD=Q11368078|lt=Maruhakayama Kofun}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}} {{ill|Gyōda|en|Gyōda|WD=Q860659|lt=Gyōda}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|en|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|WD=Q11639745|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Among early period round tumuli, it Ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|en|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|WD=Q28685132|lt=Onoe Kurumayama Kofun}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakotadōyama Kofun|qq=}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小盛山古墳|version=101786624|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{wikidata link|Q28685137}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Full view of the mound |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}} {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama|1=Okayama}} {{ill|北區|en|北區|WD=Q1180096|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|1=北區}} Hirayama |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 42|coordinates = {{Coord|34.700694|N|133.835406|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |coordinatesrees =133|omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter: 95 m |built = Late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |historical_site = None |noteworthy_features = Third-largest round tumulus in Japan |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Komoriyama Kofun |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} }} '''{{ill|Komoriyama Kofun|ja|小盛山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komoriyama Kofun|qq=}}''' (小盛山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|北區|en|北區|WD=Q1180096|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|1=北區}}, {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a round tumulus and is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. This tumulus Ranks as the third-largest round tumulus in Japan.The main round tumuli in Japan are as follows: # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}}) - Diameter: 109 meters. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|en|Maruhakayama Kofun|WD=Q11368078|lt=Maruhakayama Kofun|1=Maruhakayama Kofun}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}} {{ill|Gyōda|en|Gyōda|WD=Q860659|lt=Gyōda|1=Gyōda}}) - Diameter: 105 meters. # '''Komoriyama Kofun''' (Okayama, Okayama Prefecture) - Diameter: 95 meters. It is also known as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" (造山古墳), though it is distinct from the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|en|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|WD=Q11639745|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)}} in Shinjo-shimo, Kita-ku, Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Overview == Komoriyama Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of a hill on the western side of Okayama City.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} Its type is circular and includes a {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} (a projecting plattype). The mound is built in three tiers, while the tsukuridashi is built in two tiers. The diameter of the mound is 95 meters, and the total length, including the tsukuridashi, is 108 meters, making it the third-largest round tumulus in Japan. Haniwa and paving stones have been identified on the surface of the mound. Four reservoirs surrounding the mound are believed to be remnants of a surrounding moat, though details remain unclear. The burial facility itself is unknown.{{Sfn|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}}, during the latter part of the {{ill|Early Kofun period|ja|古墳時代前期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Among early period round tumuli, it Ranks as the largest in Japan. In the Kibi region, preceding tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|en|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|WD=Q28685132|lt=Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|1=Onoe Kurumayama Kofun}} and {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} are known for being located on hilltops overlooking the sea. In contrast, Komoriyama Kofun's location differs, drawing academic attention. It is speculated that the lineage of local chieftain burials transitioned from this kofun to later tumuli such as {{ill|Sakotadōyama Kofun|ja|佐古田堂山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakotadōyama Kofun|qq=}} and Tsukuriyama Kofun.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun2/02.html Sakotadōyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). == notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Komoriyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Komoriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/10.html Komoriyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小盛山古墳|version=101786624|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:小盛山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E7%9B%9B%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{wikidata link|Q28685137}} [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]
